title: tectaria morlae
intro: Tectaria morlae, also known as the climbing fern or the climbing water fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Tectaria morlae include the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever and digestive problems. Tinctures, infusions, and ointments are among the herbal preparations made from Tectaria morlae, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
